info.title: Anaplan Authentication Service API
info.description: Generates and manages authentication tokens for use with Anaplan REST APIs. Supports three authentication methods: HTTP Basic (username/password), CA Certificate (X.509 certificate with SHA512withRSA signature), and token refresh. Tokens expire after 35 minutes. The correct server URL depends on your Anaplan region — see the servers list below.

servers.description: US1 (Data Center - US East), US2 (Data Center - US West), US5 (Cloud - US East), US7 (Cloud - US), EU1 (Data Center - Netherlands), EU2 (Data Center - Germany), EU4 (Cloud - Europe), AP1 (Cloud - Japan)

post.description:
Minimum role: None.

Creates an AnaplanAuthToken for use with other Anaplan REST APIs. Returns 201 on success.

**HTTP Basic Auth:** Send `Authorization: Basic {base64(username:password)}` with no request body.

**CA Certificate Auth:** Send `Authorization: CACertificate {base64_encoded_certificate}` with a JSON body containing `encodedData` (base64-encoded random bytes, 100+) and `encodedSignedData` (SHA512withRSA signature of the raw random bytes, base64-encoded). The certificate must be registered for API authentication in your Anaplan instance.

**SSO workspaces:** Users must be assigned as an Exception User to use Basic authentication. See [Assign Exception Users](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Administration_and_Security/Security/Single_Sign-on/Enforcing_SAML_Authentication.html) in Anapedia.

Minimum role: None.

Extends the session for an existing AnaplanAuthToken. Tokens expire after 35 minutes; call this endpoint before expiry to maintain a session without re-authenticating.

The response returns the same `tokenValue` — the token string itself does not change, but its internal expiration is reset.

get.description:
Minimum role: None.

Returns details for a valid AnaplanAuthToken, including expiry timestamp, token ID, and user/customer identifiers. Returns 401 if the token is invalid, expired, or has been revoked by logout.

**Note:** `userGuid` and `customerGuid` are 32-character hexadecimal strings (no hyphens), not standard UUIDs.

post.description:
Minimum role: None.

Immediately invalidates an AnaplanAuthToken. Returns 204 with no body on success. Any subsequent use of the token — including validation or refresh — returns 401.

EmptyPayload.description: Used for Basic Authentication. No body parameters are accepted or required.

CertPayload.description: Request body for CA Certificate authentication. Sent together with an `Authorization: CACertificate {base64_encoded_cert}` header.

certificateChain.description: Base64-encoded certificate chain in PEM format, registered for API authentication in the Anaplan instance.

signature.description: Base64-encoded SHA512withRSA signature of the random byte string, signed with the private key for `certificateChain`.
